title: Proactive Spend Alerts and Forecasts
description:
## Getting Warned Before You Overspend

A finance team wants to be **proactively notified** when the company's monthly AWS spend is **forecasted to exceed a defined threshold**, and again when **actual** spend crosses it — so they can act before the bill closes. They also want these thresholds set per team via tags and to trigger notifications (email/SNS) automatically. Which AWS capability BEST meets this need?

options.text: AWS Budgets, configured with cost budgets, forecasted and actual alert thresholds, and SNS/email notifications
AWS Cost Explorer, reviewed weekly to look for trends
The Cost & Usage Report (CUR) delivered to S3 and queried with Athena

explanation: **AWS Budgets** is purpose-built for proactive cost governance: you define cost/usage budgets (scoped by tags, accounts, services, etc.) and set alert thresholds on **both actual and forecasted** spend, with notifications via email or Amazon SNS — exactly the "warn me before I exceed X" requirement. **Cost Explorer** (B) is an analysis/visualization tool for historical spend; it offers forecasting in its views but does not push proactive threshold alerts. **CUR** (C) is raw line-item data for querying — it has no native alerting; you'd have to build the alerting yourself. **Trusted Advisor** (D) surfaces best-practice and idle-resource checks, not budget-threshold spend alerts.

hints.title: Proactive vs analytical
hints.body: Cost Explorer and CUR tell you what happened; the requirement is to be alerted before/when a threshold is crossed. Which tool's job is alerting?

hints.title: Forecast + actual
hints.body: Look for the service that can alert on forecasted spend, not just actual.
